title: Disruption
html:
Disruption is one of Yahir's longest-running thinking lenses — it shows up at age 17 as a literal pitch-deck prompt and re-emerges in 2026 as the operating thesis behind Rankett. The earliest fingerprint is 2019 08 12 keep things simple, where he writes himself questions like "Difference between an emerging product and a disruptive product?" and "Disrupt ourselves." — the language of someone preparing a YEBW pitch but already chewing on the framework personally.

By the COVID-era 2020 03 30 facebook pandemic watchlist, disruption has become a futures lens: he's writing in all-caps about "MASS MOVE TO DIGITAL OVER BRICK AND MORTAR" and the surveillance state arriving via contact tracing — picking out structural shifts as they happen. The 2022 2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream sharpens this further with the rule "Take existing things if equivalent value, but make them with 1/10 less resources and significantly cheaper" — a practitioner's restatement of disruption theory. By 2024 05 13 things the same pattern has crystallized into a "decide on" menu of disruption theses: asset tokenization, disrupting security and wasteful human interfaces, smart-home integration, peer wager apps. Adjacent to this is the "mindless boring tasks irl" inventory (parking attendant, toll booth, security guard, museum ticket seller) — Yahir literally cataloging the human jobs he wants to dissolve into software, the direct lineage to Rankett and the .

The 2025 mobile-malware screenshots (2025 06 01 screenshot 2025 06 01 at 9 15 20 pm 44f00f66, 2025 06 01 screenshot 2025 06 01 at 9 15 26 pm 7f8b8bc5) add a meta-observation worth keeping near this concept: new platforms compress old transitions. Mobile malware ran the same novelty → profit arc that desktop malware did, just on faster tape — and Apple won the early mobile security era by being annoying in the right places (per-permission prompts). Disruption sometimes wins by being deliberately frictional at the right surface.

By 2026 01 13 rankett com, disruption is operationalized into ai search optimization — a category Yahir is staking through whitelabel strategy sold to agencies. The note where he asks "Are there people out there rn that are truly pursuing this with all of their intention and soul?" is the same lens from 2019, just with seven years of compounded conviction behind it.

Source summaries

  • 2019 08 12 keep things simple — first articulation
  • 2020 03 30 facebook pandemic watchlist — disruption-as-futures-watch
  • 2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream — "1/10 less resources" rule
  • 2024 05 13 things — disruption theses + boring-jobs inventory
  • 2025 06 01 screenshot 2025 06 01 at 9 15 20 pm 44f00f66 — platform-transition compression
  • 2025 06 01 screenshot 2025 06 01 at 9 15 26 pm 7f8b8bc5 — friction-as-disruption pattern
  • 2026 01 13 rankett com — current operating thesis

Connections

  • rankett — current vehicle
  • ai search optimization — current category bet
  • — adjacent product surface
  • nonobvious — the filter applied
  • asking the right question — the prerequisite skill
  • mindless human interface disruption — the boring-jobs subthread
  • fear of not doing — the engine behind it
